groups.title: One-day classes at Steynford College
groups.desc:
Questions 1-10
Complete the table below.
Write ONE WORD AND/OR A NUMBER for each answer.

content: It provides information on the use of herbs. There are no places at present.
There is also an extra charge for ingredients. Participants make white bread, sourdough and $22189$.
The teacher trained in $22190$. Bring a $22191$.
Only $22193$ ingredients are used. The candles can be used as presents.
Learn how to use a drill, saw and $22196$; put up a shelf.

questions.analysis: 题干询问越南菜课程的日期，女士在对话中明确提到‘I think that was on the 13th of January’，男士随后确认日期正确。虽然她最初说‘about 60 pounds’，但日期部分未被纠正，因此‘13 January’是准确答案。注意此处只需填写日期和月份，无需年份。
题干要求填写面包制作课的基础费用。男士明确说明‘The actual cost is £48’，并补充食材另收费。尽管总花费可能更高，但题目问的是课程本身的费用，因此正确答案是‘48’。注意不要误填包含食材费的估算总额。
题干要求填写面包课制作的第三种食品。男士列举了‘white bread’、‘sourdough’和‘pizza’，其中pizza是最后一项，且用‘they learn how to make pizza’明确指出属于课程内容。因此‘pizza’是唯一符合空格语境的答案。
题干询问面部按摩教师的培训地点。男士提到‘she actually did her training there’，而前文指出‘a traditional technique used in India’，‘there’指代India。因此‘India’是直接对应的答案，且符合国家名称的填写要求。
题干要求填写面部按摩课需自带的物品。男士解释‘You practise it on yourself so you have to take a mirror to the class’，明确指出需要带镜子以便观察操作。因此‘mirror’是唯一符合上下文和语法（可数名词单数）的答案。
题干询问蜡烛制作课的新日期。男士先查证后确认‘yes, it's on the 6th’，并说明原定1月23日已更改。结合上下文‘sometime in April’，可知是4月6日。因此‘6 April’是准确答案，注意格式需包含日与月。
题干要求填写蜡烛所用原料的性质。男士提到‘the candles are all made out of natural products’，‘natural products’对应题干中的‘ingredients’，因此形容词‘natural’是正确答案。注意此处只需填修饰词，而非完整短语。
题干要求填写丝绸绘画课的费用。男士直接报出‘It's £67.50’，并强调性价比高。该数字精确到小数点后两位，符合价格填写惯例。因此‘67.50’是唯一正确答案，注意不可省略小数部分或写成文字形式。
题干要求填写丝绸绘画课需自带的旧衣物。男士建议‘an apron if you've got one, or a shirt that you don't use any more’，说明shirt是围裙之外的替代选项。因此‘shirt’符合‘old clothing’的语境，且为可数名词单数。
题干要求填写DIY初学者课学习使用的工具之一。女士提到‘I even need to learn how to use a hammer’，男士回应‘Yes, you'll do that too’，确认hammer是课程内容。结合前文已提drill和saw，hammer是第三种工具，因此正确。

transcript.en: PART 1
You will hear a woman phoning to find out about one day classes held at Stainford College.
transcript.cn: 你将听到一位女士致电咨询斯坦福德学院开设的一日课程。

transcript.en: First, you have some time to look at questions 1 to 5.
transcript.cn: 首先，你有一些时间查看第1至第5题。

transcript.en: Now listen carefully and answer questions 1 to 5.
transcript.cn: 现在请仔细听，并回答第1至第5题。

transcript.en: MAN: Hello, Steynford College external course registration, can I help you?
transcript.cn: 男士：你好，斯坦福学院外部课程注册处，请问有什么可以帮到您？

transcript.en: WOMAN: Yeah, I'm ringing to find out about one-day classes next year. I got a leaflet about them in the post but I lost it, and I understand some of the classes are filling up fast, so I might need to book quite soon if I want to go ahead.
transcript.cn: 女士：是的，我打电话是想了解明年的一日课程。我收到了一份关于这些课程的传单，但丢失了，而且我了解到有些课程报名很快，所以如果我想参加可能需要尽快预订。

transcript.en: MAN: Sure. Can you remember which one you were considering?
transcript.cn: 男士：当然，您能记得您在考虑哪个课程吗？

transcript.en: WOMAN: There were a few actually. I remember there was one on how to make Vietnamese food that sounded good. I think that was on the 13th of January. It cost about 60 pounds.
transcript.cn: 女士：其实有好几个。我记得有一个关于如何制作越南食物的，听起来不错。我想是在1月13日，费用大约60英镑。

transcript.en: MAN: Yes, you're right about the date, but it's 59 pounds actually. It's a very popular class, and among other things the teacher explains how Vietnamese food includes lots of different herbs. I'm afraid that all the places are taken at present, but I can put you on the waiting list if you want?
transcript.cn: 男士：是的，您说的日期没错，但费用实际上是59英镑。这是一门非常受欢迎的课，其中老师会讲解越南食物如何包含许多不同的香草。很遗憾目前所有名额都已满，但如果您愿意，我可以将您加入候补名单。

transcript.en: WOMAN: No, that's OK. I'm quite interested in the bread making class. That's in March sometime, isn't it?
transcript.cn: 女士：不用了，没关系。我对烘焙面包课程很感兴趣，那是在三月份的某个时候，对吗？

transcript.en: MAN: Yeah, the 20th of March. Would you like to register for that?
transcript.cn: 男士：是的，3月20日。您想报名参加吗？

transcript.en: WOMAN: I'm not sure. How much is it?
transcript.cn: 女士：我还不确定，费用是多少？

transcript.en: MAN: The actual cost is £48 but then there's an extra charge as well as that for the ingredients – I'm not sure how much that is, no more than twenty pounds I think.
transcript.cn: 男士：实际费用是48英镑，但还有额外的食材费用——我不确定具体金额，我想不超过20英镑。

transcript.en: WOMAN: So what sorts of things do they make in the class?
transcript.cn: 女士：那在课上会做哪些东西？

transcript.en: MAN: Oh, various types of bread; I think they make white bread and then they make sourdough, that seems to be very fashionable at present, and they learn how to make pizza, which is apparently really good.
transcript.cn: 男士：哦，各种类型的面包；我想他们会做白面包，然后做酸面包——这目前似乎非常流行，他们还会学如何做披萨，据说效果非常好。

transcript.en: WOMAN: Well I'd definitely be interested in that, but there were also a few other classes that sounded interesting. I think there was one on face massage? I'd love to learn how to do that.
transcript.cn: 女士：嗯，我肯定对那个感兴趣，但还有一些其他听起来很有趣的课程。我想有一个关于面部按摩的课程？我很想学习如何做。

transcript.en: MAN: Yeah, that's on the 23rd of February and it costs just £35 for the day. The teacher's great, the type of massage done is a traditional technique used in India and she actually did her training there. The massage is meant to relax you and get rid of lines and wrinkles. You practise it on yourself so you have to take a mirror to the class, so you can see what you're doing.
transcript.cn: 男士：是的，那是在2月23日，一天只需35英镑。老师很棒，所做的按摩是印度传统技术，她实际上在那里接受过培训。按摩的目的是让您放松并消除皱纹和细纹。您在自己身上练习，所以需要带一面镜子去上课，这样您可以看到自己在做什么。

transcript.en: WOMAN: OK.
transcript.cn: 女士：好的。

transcript.en: Before you hear the rest of the conversation, you have some time to look at questions 6 to 10.
transcript.cn: 在你听剩余对话之前，你有一些时间查看第6至第10题。

transcript.en: Now listen and answer questions 6 to 10.
transcript.cn: 现在请听录音，并回答第6至第10题。

transcript.en: WOMAN: And I think there was a class in candle making?
transcript.cn: 女士：我想还有一个制作蜡烛的课程？

transcript.en: MAN: Yes that's sometime in April, I think. Let me check … yes, it's on the 6th. It was on the 23rd of January but they had to change the date. It's just £52. That's a popular course too. I think one reason why people like it is because the candles are all made out of natural products. It's filling up fast but there's still a few places left.
transcript.cn: 男士：是的，我想是在四月份，让我查一下……是的，在6日。原来定在1月23日，但他们不得不更改日期，只需52英镑。这也是一门受欢迎的课程，我想人们喜欢它的原因之一是蜡烛全部由天然产品制成。报名很快，但还有几个名额。

transcript.en: WOMAN: Yes one of my friends did that class. She said the candles make really good presents. In fact she gave me one.
transcript.cn: 女士：是的，我的一个朋友上过那门课，她说蜡烛是很好的礼物，事实上她送了我一个。

transcript.en: MAN: By the way, have you heard about the class on silk painting? That's being held on the 18th of May. You learn how to create designs on silk fabric and colour them using special dyes. Apparently people can produce beautiful artworks that way, either to put on the wall as a picture or to use for something like a scarf. It's £67.50, which is really good value I think – there's a similar class I've seen that was £110.
transcript.cn: 男士：顺便问一下，您听说过丝绸绘画课吗？那是在5月18日举行。您将学习如何在丝绸织物上创作图案，并使用特殊染料为其上色。据说这样可以创作出美丽的艺术品，既可以挂在墙上作为画作，也可以用作围巾之类的东西。费用是67.50英镑，我觉得非常划算——我见过类似的课程要110英镑。

transcript.en: WOMAN: That sounds interesting. Would I need to bring the silk?
transcript.cn: 女士：听起来很有趣，我需要自带丝绸吗？

transcript.en: MAN: No, the only thing is everyone has to bring something to protect their clothing, like an apron if you've got one, or a shirt that you don't use any more, because the dye can really stain your clothes.
transcript.cn: 男士：不需要，唯一要带的是用来保护衣物的东西，比如您有的话可以带围裙，或者一件不再穿的旧衬衫，因为染料真的会染污您的衣服。

transcript.en: WOMAN: Right. Then the last class I was considering was a bit different, that was on DIY for beginners. I'd like to learn how to do household repairs. Are there any places left on that?
transcript.cn: 女士：好的。最后一个我在考虑的课程有点不同，是面向初学者的家居维修课，我想学习如何做家居维修，那个课程还有名额吗？

transcript.en: MAN: That's on the 24th of February – yes, there are a few places. It's a bit more expensive – it's £125 – but it's a very popular class. You learn how to use an electric drill and a saw.
transcript.cn: 男士：那是在2月24日——是的，还有几个名额。费用稍贵——125英镑——但这是一门非常受欢迎的课程，您将学习如何使用电钻和锯子。

transcript.en: WOMAN: Yeah, that would be really useful, and I even need to learn how to use a hammer because I always end up hitting my fingers.
transcript.cn: 女士：是的，那会非常有用，我甚至需要学习如何使用锤子，因为我总是会敲到自己的手指。

transcript.en: MAN: Yes, you'll do that too. And when you've learned how to use the basic tools, you do a practical job which is fixing a shelf to a wall.
transcript.cn: 男士：是的，您也会学到这个。当您学会使用基本工具后，会进行一项实践作业，就是将搁板固定在墙上。

transcript.en: WOMAN: Great. Just what I need to know. Right, well I'd like to enrol for that and also for …
transcript.cn: 女士：太好了，正是我需要学的。好的，我想报名参加那个课程，还有……

groups.desc:
Questions 11 and 12
Choose TWO letters, A-E.

list.title: A
list.content: Stop for lunch in an ancient town.

list.title: B
list.content: Do not miss the ruins of a certain building.

list.title: C
list.content: Catch a boat to the start of this walk.

list.title: D
list.content: Be careful of the steep and rocky paths.

list.title: E
list.content: Do not worry about getting lost.

collect_option.title: Which TWO pieces of advice are given about the Marsden Coastal Walk?

questions.analysis: 题目要求选出关于 Marsden Coastal Walk 的两条建议。原文提到路线会经过一座建于1400年代、现已只剩石堆的城堡（对应选项B“不要错过某建筑的遗迹”），还强调沿途有清晰路标，甚至无需带地图（对应选项E“不用担心迷路”）。其他选项如A、C、D在文中均未提及，因此正确答案是B和E。
此题与第11题共用题干，同样考查 Marsden Coastal Walk 的建议。原文明确指出该路线经过一处已成废墟的城堡（即“ruins of a certain building”，对应B），并说明因有清晰标识而不需地图（即“do not worry about getting lost”，对应E）。其余选项如乘船出发、陡峭路径或在古镇午餐均未出现，故答案仍为B和E。

groups.desc:
Questions 13 and 14
Choose TWO letters, A-E.

list.title: A
list.content: This walk is mostly downhill.

list.title: B
list.content: The paths can get busy during the day.

list.title: C
list.content: This is a circular walk.

list.title: D
list.content: A tower stands on the site of an older structure.

list.title: E
list.content: There are far-reaching views the whole way.

collect_option.title: Which TWO things are said about the Melby Heritage Walk?

questions.analysis: 题目要求选出关于 Melby Heritage Walk 的两个正确描述。原文提到路线最终回到起点——Melby Coal Mine 的停车场（对应C“这是一条环形步道”），还说明塔楼建在曾经的17世纪狩猎小屋原址上（对应D“塔楼位于更早建筑的旧址”）。其他选项如全程视野开阔、主要下坡或白天人多，在文中均无依据，因此正确答案是C和D。

此题与第13题同属一组，考查 Melby Heritage Walk 的特点。听力中明确说路线“bring you back down to the starting point”（即环形路线，对应C），且塔楼“situated where there was once a seventeenth century hunting lodge”（即建于旧结构之上，对应D）。选项A、B、E虽看似合理，但原文未提及其路径坡度、人流或全程视野，故答案为C和D。

groups.desc:
Questions 15-20
Label the map below.
Write the correct letter, A-I, next to Questions 15-20.

questions.title: Exhibition
questions.analysis: 题目要求标注展览（Exhibition）的位置。原文说明展览位于主访客中心内，从停车场经有顶通道（covered walkway）抵达（句子21-22）。结合地图选项，F位置符合“从停车场经有顶通道到达主访客中心”的描述，因此答案为F。

questions.title: Baths
questions.analysis: 题目询问浴室（Baths）的位置。原文明确指出浴房位于引擎房正北的建筑内（“directly to the north of the engine house”，句子23）。根据地图方位，B点正好处于引擎房以北，因此正确答案是B。

questions.title: Tools
questions.analysis: 题目要求定位工具展（Tools）的位置。原文提到早期采矿工具陈列在“site北部边界中部的一个L形小建筑内”（句子25）。地图中D点位于北侧边界中央区域，符合“middle of the northern boundary”的描述，因此答案为D。

questions.title: Vehicles
questions.analysis: 题目询问车辆棚（Vehicles）的位置。原文说明车辆棚位于“场地西南角，可通过有顶通道进入”（句子27）。地图中A点处于西南角，且连接有顶通道，完全匹配描述，因此正确答案是A。

questions.title: Ponies
questions.analysis: 题目要求标注小马场（Ponies）的位置。原文指出小马场位于“场地东北边界，且不通过有顶通道到达”（句子28）。地图中H点位于东北角，且未连接有顶通道，符合所有细节，因此答案为H。

questions.title: Education centre
questions.analysis: 题目询问教育中心（Education centre）的位置。原文说明该中心“通过一段短的有顶通道与售票处相连”（句子30）。地图中E点紧邻售票处，并由短廊道连接，完全符合描述，因此正确答案是E。

transcript.en: PART 2
You will hear some recorded information for visitors to a region about history and nature walks there, and about a particular visitor attraction.
transcript.cn: 你将听到一段面向游客的录音信息，内容是某地区的历史与自然步道，以及一个特定的游客景点。

transcript.en: First, you have some time to look at questions 11 to 14.
transcript.cn: 首先，你有一些时间查看第11至第14题。

transcript.en: Now listen carefully and answer questions 11 to 14.
transcript.cn: 现在请仔细听，并回答第11至第14题。

transcript.en: Here is some information about history and nature walks in the region.
transcript.cn: 以下是该地区历史与自然徒步活动的一些信息。

transcript.en: They can be completed in less than a day and also be combined with a visit to Melby, a former coal mine now open to the public.
transcript.cn: 这些活动可以在不到一天的时间内完成，也可以与参观梅尔比（一个现已对公众开放的前煤矿）相结合。

transcript.en: The Marsden Coastal Walk is suitable for all the family as there are no strenuous climbs.
transcript.cn: 马斯登海岸徒步适合全家参与，因为没有费力的攀爬路段。

transcript.en: The route begins at Marsden harbour, where there are hourly ferries to and from the beautiful Hooker Island, a great place for fishing.
transcript.cn: 路线从马斯登港口开始，那里有每小时一班的渡轮往返于美丽的胡克岛，是钓鱼的好地方。

transcript.en: The walk covers part of the coastal path trail. As there are clear signposts all along the way, you don't even need to take a map with you.
transcript.cn: 这次徒步涵盖了海岸小径的一部分。由于沿途有清晰的路标，您甚至不需要带地图。

transcript.en: At one point, the route goes inland slightly and passes a castle built in the 1400s near to a now-vanished market town.
transcript.cn: 路线在某处稍微向内陆延伸，经过一座建于1400年代的城堡，旁边曾有一个现已消失的集镇。

transcript.en: The castle's now just a pile of stones but it's a great place to take photos, and fascinating information boards show what it once looked like.
transcript.cn: 城堡现在只是一堆石头，但这是拍照的好地方，迷人的信息板展示了它曾经的样子。

transcript.en: If you set off early, you can be back in time for midday.
transcript.cn: 如果您早出发，可以在中午前回来。

transcript.en: The Melby Heritage Walk is a great place to take photos, especially of the night sky. At certain times of the year, people come here from far and wide. They climb to the top of the valley and take pictures of the stars.
transcript.cn: 梅尔比遗产徒步是拍照的好地方，尤其是拍夜空。在一年中的某些时候，人们从四面八方来到这里，爬到山谷顶部拍摄星星。

transcript.en: In the daytime, it's completely different. As you hike through the dense woods in the valley bottom, the only things you'll hear are the sound of your own footsteps.
transcript.cn: 白天则完全不同。当您徒步穿越山谷底部的茂密森林时，您唯一能听到的就是自己的脚步声。

transcript.en: At the highest point, you can stop to take in the views, and those with lots of energy can climb the tower that's situated where there was once a seventeenth century hunting lodge.
transcript.cn: 在最高点，您可以停下来欣赏美景，精力充沛的人还可以爬上一座塔楼，那里曾经是一座17世纪的狩猎小屋。

transcript.en: The route continues along the tops of the hills and brings you back down to the starting point – the car park at Melby Coal Mine.
transcript.cn: 路线沿山顶继续，将您带回出发点——梅尔比煤矿的停车场。

transcript.en: Before you hear the rest of the recorded information, you have some time to look at questions 15 to 20.
transcript.cn: 在你听录音信息的剩余部分之前，你有一些时间查看第15至第20题。

transcript.en: Now listen and answer questions 15 to 20.
transcript.cn: 现在请听录音，并回答第15至第20题。

transcript.en: Melby Coal Mine has been open to the public for twenty years and has won awards for its visitor experience. Many of the buildings around the mine are still standing and have been converted into display areas.
transcript.cn: 梅尔比煤矿已向公众开放二十年，并因其参观体验获得了多个奖项。矿区周围的许多建筑仍然存在，已被改建为展示区。

transcript.en: Firstly, there's an exhibition showing the history of the mine, with many original black and white photos.
transcript.cn: 首先，有一个展览展示了煤矿的历史，包含许多原始黑白照片。

transcript.en: To see that, you go from the car park, via a covered walkway to the Main Visitor Centre.
transcript.cn: 要参观该展览，您需要从停车场经过有顶棚的走廊前往主游客中心。

transcript.en: Go through the ticket office to an area where there are lockers to leave heavy bags in, and where you can borrow raincoats. Beyond that room is the exhibition.
transcript.cn: 穿过售票处，到达一个可以寄存重行李的储物柜区域，在那里您还可以借用雨衣。那个房间再往里就是展览区。

transcript.en: There's a small bathhouse where miners used to wash after their shift underground. You can see that in the building directly to the north of the engine house.
transcript.cn: 有一个小澡堂，矿工们曾在地下换班后在那里洗澡。您可以在发动机房正北方向的建筑中看到它。

transcript.en: The boiler has gone from there now, but there are lines of tin baths on the stone floor.
transcript.cn: 锅炉已经不在那里了，但石地板上仍有一排排的铁皮浴盆。

transcript.en: There's a display of early mining tools from the days before mechanisation. You can find that in a small L-shaped building in the middle of the northern boundary of the site.
transcript.cn: 有一个展示机械化之前时代早期采矿工具的展区。您可以在场地北部边界中间的一栋小型L形建筑中找到它。

transcript.en: It's incredible to think how miners were able to use hand implements to cut through rock.
transcript.cn: 想想矿工们如何能够用手工工具凿穿岩石，真是令人难以置信。

transcript.en: The vehicle shed, where you can find wagons of different sizes, along with some of the hi-tech cutting machines that were in use until the mine closed, is in the southwest corner of the site, and can be accessed via a covered walkway.
transcript.cn: 车辆库里有各种尺寸的货车，以及一些直到煤矿关闭前还在使用的高科技切割机，位于场地西南角，可通过有顶棚的走廊进入。

transcript.en: There is a field with ponies, which are always popular with children, on the north-eastern boundary of the site, not approached via a covered walkway.
transcript.cn: 场地东北边界有一片有小马的草地，小马深受孩子们喜爱，不经由有顶棚的走廊到达。

transcript.en: The other building to mention is the education centre.
transcript.cn: 另一个值得一提的建筑是教育中心。

transcript.en: This is where school groups go when they visit the mine, but it's also accessible to the public as it contains a library and small gift shop. The centre is connected to the ticket office via a short section of covered walkway.
transcript.cn: 这是学校团体参观煤矿时前往的地方，但也向公众开放，因为里面有图书馆和小型礼品店。该中心通过一小段有顶棚的走廊与售票处相连。

groups.desc:
Questions 21 and 22
Choose TWO letters, A-E.

list.title: A
list.content: the amount of plastic in the ocean

list.title: B
list.content: the number of diseases caused by contaminated food

list.title: C
list.content: the amount of food that is wasted

list.title: D
list.content: the number of people who are obese

list.title: E
list.content: the result of treating animals with antibiotics

collect_option.title: Which TWO facts in the sessions on food safety were new information for Nadia and Fergus?

questions.analysis: 题目要求选出Nadia和Fergus在食品安全课程中首次了解到的两个事实。原文中，Fergus表示他‘ hadn’t realised that unsafe food causes more than 200 different diseases’（选项B），而Nadia和Fergus都对‘抗生素用于动物后进入食物链导致耐药性增加’感到惊讶（选项E）。虽然肥胖率上升（D）和塑料污染（A）也被提及，但说话人明确表示这些是他们已知的信息，因此正确答案为B和E。
此题与第21题共享题干，同样要求选出两人此前不知道的两个事实。对话中，Fergus对不安全食品引发200多种疾病表示惊讶（对应B），而两人均对动物使用抗生素后进入食物链导致耐药性上升感到意外（对应E）。其他选项如肥胖（D）和食物浪费（C）虽被提到，但前者是Nadia已知的，后者是Fergus认为课程讲得不够深入而非新信息，故答案仍为B和E。

groups.desc:
Questions 23 and 24
Choose TWO letters, A-E.

list.title: A
list.content: the new technology it used

list.title: B
list.content: the publicity it received

list.title: C
list.content: the use of multiple tests on food items

list.title: D
list.content: the variety of dietary requirements included

list.title: E
list.content: the way information was made widely accessible

collect_option.title: Which TWO features of a project aiming to prevent food fraud impressed Fergus?

questions.analysis: 题目问Fergus对防止食品欺诈项目的哪两点印象深刻。他提到研究人员在食品从生产者到消费者的多个阶段进行DNA检测（对应C，“multiple tests”），并强调该项目关注了宗教饮食规定、素食者、纯素者及过敏人群的需求（对应D，“variety of dietary requirements”）。虽然他也提到宣传存在问题，因此B和E不成立；A项“新技术”未被特别强调，故正确答案为C和D。
本题与第23题共用题干，继续考查Fergus赞赏的项目特点。他明确指出检测贯穿食品供应链各环节（对应C），且项目覆盖了多种特殊饮食群体（对应D）。尽管提到了DNA分析技术，但重点在于其应用方式而非技术本身（排除A）；他还批评了宣传效果（排除B和E）。因此，正确选项仍是C和D。

groups.desc:
Questions 25 and 26
Choose TWO letters, A-E.

list.title: A
list.content: sustainable fishing

list.title: B
list.content: targeted nutrition

list.title: C
list.content: global differences in consumption

list.title: D
list.content: sustainable agriculture

list.title: E
list.content: digital technology and food

collect_option.title: Which TWO topics do both students recommend should be included in the course?

questions.analysis: 题目要求选出两位学生都推荐加入课程的两个主题。Fergus提议关注‘seafood industry’以避免过度捕捞（对应A“sustainable fishing”），Nadia随后赞同；接着她建议探讨‘全球各地的食物和饮食习惯差异’（对应C），Fergus也表示全班都会感兴趣。其他选项如精准营养（B）仅Nadia提出，Fergus认为偏医学；可持续农业（D）和数字技术（E）则未获双方共同支持，故答案为A和C。
此题与第25题一致，需找出两人共同推荐的主题。Fergus主张增加关于渔业资源保护的内容（对应A），Nadia同意；之后她提出研究全球饮食差异（对应C），Fergus积极回应。而靶向营养（B）被Fergus质疑不属于食品科学范畴，数字技术（E）他认为太专业，可持续农业（D）则是已学内容。因此，唯一双方都认可的是A和C。

groups.title: Comments
groups.question_title: Student project: developing a new food product
groups.desc:
Questions 27-30
Complete the flow-chart below.
Choose FOUR answers from the box and write the correct letter, A-F, next to Questions 27-30.

list.title: A
list.content: This was challenging but enjoyable.

list.title: B
list.content: This led to some disagreement.

list.title: C
list.content: This was easy to decide on.

list.title: D
list.content: This was helped by the guidelines provided.

list.title: E
list.content: This seemed like an unnecessary stage.

list.title: F
list.content: This involved selecting a new ingredient.

questions.title: Initial aim
questions.analysis: 题目要求为‘Initial aim’选择对应描述。Fergus提到他们‘made up our minds about that pretty quickly’（很快决定了目标），并对比其他同学花费大量时间难以抉择，说明该阶段容易决定。选项C‘This was easy to decide on’准确概括了这一信息，而其他选项如‘disagreement’或‘unnecessary’均未在对话中体现，故选C。

questions.title: Literature review
questions.analysis: 关于‘Literature review’阶段，Nadia明确表示他们此前从未做过文献综述，而项目提供的‘handout with advice’非常有用。这直接对应选项D‘This was helped by the guidelines provided’。其他选项如‘challenging’或‘disagreement’未被提及，且Fergus补充说指导材料对信息呈现也很有帮助，进一步支持D为正确答案。

questions.title: Product development
questions.analysis: ‘Product development’阶段指确定falafel的具体配方。Nadia提到他们最终决定使用jackfruit（一种两人从未尝过的水果），Fergus也强调想做出与普通falafel不同的产品。这明显属于‘selecting a new ingredient’，对应选项F。其他选项如‘disagreement’或‘unnecessary’均无依据，故正确答案为F。

questions.title: Product production
questions.analysis: ‘Product production’指实际制作falafel的过程。Fergus称其‘harder than I expected’，Nadia则描述为‘mostly trial and error’，但最后两人一致表示‘had a lot of fun’。这完美契合选项A‘This was challenging but enjoyable’。其他选项如‘led to disagreement’或‘seemed unnecessary’在对话中完全未出现，因此选A。

transcript.en: PART 3
You will hear two food science students called Nadia and Fergus talking to their tutor about their course.
transcript.cn: 你将听到两名食品科学学生娜迪娅和弗格斯与导师讨论他们的课程。

transcript.en: First, you have some time to look at questions 21 to 26.
transcript.cn: 首先，你有一些时间查看第21至第26题。

transcript.en: Now listen carefully and answer questions 21 to 26.
transcript.cn: 现在请仔细听，并回答第21至第26题。

transcript.en: TUTOR: Thanks for coming along, Nadia and Fergus. So this is a chance for you to give us some feedback about different aspects of your course. What would you like to begin with?
transcript.cn: 导师：感谢你们过来，纳迪亚和弗格斯。这是一个机会让你们对课程的不同方面给我们一些反馈，你们想从哪方面开始？

transcript.en: NADIA: I've enjoyed the sessions on food safety. There was some information there that I found really surprising, although some was stuff I knew already, like the rise in rates of obesity.
transcript.cn: 纳迪亚：我很喜欢食品安全方面的课程。其中有些信息让我非常惊讶，尽管有些是我已经知道的，比如肥胖率的上升。

transcript.en: FERGUS: Yeah, that's been in the news a lot. But I hadn't realised that unsafe food causes more than 200 different diseases.
transcript.cn: 弗格斯：是的，这在新闻中报道了很多。但我没有意识到不安全的食物会导致200多种不同的疾病。

transcript.en: NADIA: No, I'd no idea it was that many. And speaking of diseases, I knew resistance to antibiotics is on the increase, but I didn't know why...
transcript.cn: 纳迪亚：不，我完全不知道有那么多。说到疾病，我知道抗生素耐药性在增加，但我不知道为什么……

transcript.en: FERGUS: …that it's partly because when animals are treated with antibiotics then consumed by humans, the antibiotics get into the food chain. I had no idea about that either.
transcript.cn: 弗格斯：……这部分是因为当动物接受抗生素治疗后被人类食用，抗生素就进入了食物链。我也对此一无所知。

transcript.en: NADIA: Then the sessions provided a lot of information about plastic pollution from food packaging in the ocean, but I think that most of us were already aware of that.
transcript.cn: 纳迪亚：然后课程提供了大量关于食品包装海洋塑料污染的信息，但我认为我们大多数人已经知道这一点了。

transcript.en: FERGUS: Yeah. But I thought we could have done more on how much food is thrown away unnecessarily through fear of it being out of date, that was only mentioned in passing.
transcript.cn: 弗格斯：是的。但我认为我们本可以更多地讨论由于担心过期而不必要地丢弃了多少食物，这只是顺带提到了一下。

transcript.en: TUTOR: OK, I'll bear that in mind. What did you think about the sessions from visiting lecturers, Fergus?
transcript.cn: 导师：好的，我会记住这点的。弗格斯，你觉得客座讲师的课程怎么样？

transcript.en: FERGUS: For me the most interesting one was about that project to prevent companies giving incorrect information to consumers about food.
transcript.cn: 弗格斯：对我来说最有趣的是关于阻止公司向消费者提供关于食品不实信息的项目。

transcript.en: TUTOR: Ah, food fraud, yes.
transcript.cn: 导师：啊，食品欺诈，是的。

transcript.en: FERGUS: I thought it was really good to address a problem that's faced by so many different groups – people with special religious rules, as well as vegetarians and vegans.
transcript.cn: 弗格斯：我认为解决如此多不同群体面临的问题真的很好——有特殊宗教规定的人，以及素食者和纯素食者。

transcript.en: NADIA: And those with allergies.
transcript.cn: 纳迪亚：还有过敏人群。

transcript.en: FERGUS: Yeah. And another thing, we've had effective ways of analysing DNA for some time now and these can easily be applied to analysing food.
transcript.cn: 弗格斯：是的。另外，我们已经有了分析DNA的有效方法，这些方法可以很容易地应用于食品分析。

transcript.en: But what the researchers succeeded in doing was to ensure that these tests were carried out at different stages in the food's journey from the producer to the consumer.
transcript.cn: 但研究人员成功做到的是确保这些测试在食品从生产者到消费者的旅程的不同阶段进行。

transcript.en: NADIA: So they knew that the food actually came from the place it was supposed to, and had the ingredients it was meant to.
transcript.cn: 纳迪亚：这样他们就知道食物实际上来自应该来自的地方，并含有应该含有的成分。

transcript.en: FERGUS: Exactly. So customers can be confident about what they buy.
transcript.cn: 弗格斯：正是如此，所以顾客可以对他们购买的东西有信心。

transcript.en: NADIA: And the researchers had a good system for publicising their findings too.
transcript.cn: 纳迪亚：研究人员也有一套很好的系统来公布他们的发现。

transcript.en: FERGUS: Well, I thought there were some problems with that, actually.
transcript.cn: 弗格斯：嗯，我认为实际上那方面存在一些问题。

transcript.en: TUTOR: OK. And do you have any recommendations for new topics that we could include in the course?
transcript.cn: 导师：好的，你们对可以纳入课程的新主题有什么建议吗？

transcript.en: NADIA: Well, I'm interested in how crop yields can be increased without damaging the environment.
transcript.cn: 纳迪亚：嗯，我对如何在不损害环境的情况下提高作物产量感兴趣。

transcript.en: FERGUS: But we've already done quite a bit on that – but not so much on the seafood industry, where stocks are in danger of being overexploited as a food source unless we can find ways of keeping stocks up.
transcript.cn: 弗格斯：但我们已经涉及了相当多这方面的内容——但在海鲜行业方面涉及不多，该行业的储量面临被过度开发作为食物来源的危险，除非我们能找到维持储量的方法。

transcript.en: NADIA: Yes, that'd be a good topic. And I'm interested in the idea of a personalised approach to diet, now we have the technology to analyse exactly what individuals need.
transcript.cn: 纳迪亚：是的，那将是个好主题。我对个性化饮食方法的想法感兴趣，现在我们有技术可以准确分析个人需要什么。

transcript.en: FERGUS: That sounds more like a medical topic than food science.
transcript.cn: 弗格斯：那听起来更像是医学主题而非食品科学。

transcript.en: NADIA: OK. What about sessions on the variety of food and eating habits around the world? That's very relevant nowadays.
transcript.cn: 纳迪亚：好的，那么关于世界各地食物多样性和饮食习惯的课程怎么样？这在当今非常相关。

transcript.en: FERGUS: Yes, I think the whole class would be interested in that.
transcript.cn: 弗格斯：是的，我认为全班同学都会对此感兴趣。

transcript.en: NADIA: Then there's technological stuff, things like 3D printing of food and smart packaging.
transcript.cn: 纳迪亚：然后还有技术方面的内容，比如食品3D打印和智能包装。

transcript.en: FERGUS: Mmm – maybe too specialist.
transcript.cn: 弗格斯：嗯——也许太专业了。

transcript.en: Before you hear the rest of the discussion, you have some time to look at questions 27 to 30.
transcript.cn: 在你听剩余讨论之前，你有一些时间查看第27至第30题。

transcript.en: TUTOR: Now, I'm particularly interested in your project – the one where you developed a new food product. So talk me through the stages… first you had to decide on your initial aim.
transcript.cn: 导师：现在，我对你们的项目特别感兴趣——就是你们开发新食品的那个项目，请带我了解各个阶段……首先你们必须确定最初的目标。

transcript.en: FERGUS: We decided we wanted to create something people could eat on the go rather than in a restaurant.
transcript.cn: 弗格斯：我们决定想创造一种人们可以在外出时吃的东西，而不是在餐厅里吃的。

transcript.en: NADIA: Yeah so we chose falafel, which was originally a Middle Eastern snack.
transcript.cn: 纳迪亚：是的，所以我们选择了法拉费尔，这是一种起源于中东的小吃。

transcript.en: FERGUS: We made up our minds about that pretty quickly. I know some students found it a lot harder to choose, and wasted a lot of time.
transcript.cn: 弗格斯：我们很快就做出了决定。我知道有些学生发现选择更难，浪费了很多时间。

transcript.en: NADIA: Then we had to do the literature review. We hadn't done one of these before so the handout with advice for the project was very useful here.
transcript.cn: 纳迪亚：然后我们必须进行文献综述。我们之前没有做过这样的工作，所以关于项目建议的讲义在这里非常有用。

transcript.en: FERGUS: Yes, especially the advice on how to present the information. Then product development, actually deciding what we'd use to make the falafel, and for me the interesting thing about that was that we wanted it to be something a bit different from an ordinary falafel.
transcript.cn: 弗格斯：是的，尤其是关于如何呈现信息的建议。然后是产品开发，实际上决定我们用什么来制作法拉费尔，对我来说有趣的是，我们希望它与普通的法拉费尔有所不同。

transcript.en: NADIA: We really made the right choice when we finally decided to use jackfruit, even though it wasn't something that either of us had ever tasted before.
transcript.cn: 纳迪亚：当我们最终决定使用菠萝蜜时，我们真的做出了正确的选择，尽管这是我们两个人都从未尝过的东西。

transcript.en: FERGUS: Yeah, like the name tells you, it's a fruit but actually it's really good in savoury dishes. The product production, working out how to make the falafel, was harder than I expected because I'd never made them before.
transcript.cn: 弗格斯：是的，正如名字所示，它是一种水果，但实际上在咸味菜肴中非常好吃。产品生产，即研究如何制作法拉费尔，比我预期的要难，因为我以前从未做过。

transcript.en: NADIA: It was mostly trial and error. We started off with the basic recipe and then experimented and when it went wrong…
transcript.cn: 纳迪亚：主要是反复试验。我们从基本食谱开始，然后进行实验，当出错时……

transcript.en: FERGUS: …which it did a lot of the time…
transcript.cn: 弗格斯：……这经常发生……

transcript.en: NADIA: …we just moved on and kept adapting it and in the end it turned out fine and we had a lot of fun.
transcript.cn: 纳迪亚：……我们就继续并不断调整，最终结果很好，我们玩得很开心。

transcript.en: FERGUS: We did!
transcript.cn: 弗格斯：确实如此！

transcript.en: TUTOR: Thank you. Well, your project was a very good ….
transcript.cn: 导师：谢谢你们，你们的项目非常好……

groups.title: Challenges facing the cruise ship industry
groups.desc:
Questions 31-40
Complete the notes below.
Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.
groups.collect: Challenges facing the cruise ship industry Problems with overtourism $22217$ is one of the worst problems. A tourist $22218$ is being introduced in some cities to reduce numbers, e.g. Barcelona. Bruges: action was taken to limit day trips from the nearby port because the city was becoming a theme park (e.g. many shops were only stocking $22219$ and souvenirs). Dubrovnik: limits the number of tourists by managing the $22220$ of cruise ship arrivals. Problems of perception Cruises are generally associated with the elderly. There is an assumption about the $22221$ of cruises. People think there may be too many $22222$. Solutions Cruise lines are attracting younger customers by: - becoming more sustainable e.g. using hybrid engines. - having a wide range of activities e.g. boxing, $22223$ and well-being programmes. - offering a diverse selection of food including $22224$ options. - providing reliable $22225$. - improving marketing on social media with high quality $22226$.

questions.analysis: 题干要求填写过度旅游（overtourism）带来的最严重问题之一。听力原文第7句明确指出“Pollution, for example, is among the greatest threats to many popular tourist destinations”，其中“greatest threats”与题干中的“worst problems”构成同义替换，直接锁定答案为“Pollution”。该词为不可数名词，符合题目“ONE WORD ONLY”的要求。
题目询问某些城市为减少游客数量而引入的措施。原文第11句提到巴塞罗那对邮轮游客征收“a tax which all visitors... have to pay”，此处“tax”即为所实施的具体政策。题干中“tourist ___”暗示需填入一个名词，且后文以巴塞罗那为例，与原文完全对应，因此答案为“tax”。
题干描述布鲁日的商店只售卖某种商品和纪念品。原文第14句明确说明这些店铺“selling chocolate, which Belgium is famous for, and other souvenirs”，其中“chocolate”与“souvenirs”并列，且被强调为比利时著名特产。因此，“chocolate”是唯一符合语境和逻辑的答案，且为单数不可数名词，满足题目要求。

题目问杜布罗夫尼克如何限制游客数量。原文第16句指出该城“control the timing of all cruise ship entries to the city's port”，即通过控制邮轮入港的“timing”来管理人流。题干中“managing the ___ of cruise ship arrivals”与原文“control the timing”形成同义转述，因此答案为“timing”。

题干要求填写人们对邮轮的一种误解或假设。原文第19句提到“cost is also a major factor in putting younger groups off going on a cruise”，说明年轻人认为邮轮价格过高。题干中“assumption about the ___ of cruises”对应原文对成本的担忧，因此答案为“cost”，符合“ONE WORD ONLY”且为不可数名词。
题目指出人们认为邮轮上规矩太多。原文第21句提到“another perception is that cruises have lots of rules about what to wear and how to behave”，直接说明公众对邮轮存在“rules过多”的刻板印象。题干中“too many ___”需填复数名词，而“rules”正是原文关键词，因此答案为“rules”。

题干列举吸引年轻顾客的船上活动，包括拳击、___和健康项目。原文第25句提到“There's even a boxing ring on one ship, and most offer diving expeditions”，其中“diving expeditions”简化为“diving”即可满足“ONE WORD ONLY”的要求，且与boxing和well-being programmes并列，逻辑通顺。
题目要求填写邮轮为迎合年轻人口味提供的食物选项。原文第27句明确说“Vegan dishes are standard”，说明纯素食已成为标配。题干中“including ___ options”需填形容词修饰options，而“vegan”正是描述饮食类型的正确词汇，符合语法和上下文。
题干询问邮轮为满足年轻人需求而提供的可靠服务。原文第29句指出“companies have to guarantee wifi that can be relied upon at all times”，强调稳定网络对工作和联系的重要性。题干中“reliable ___”与原文“wifi that can be relied upon”直接对应，因此答案为“wifi”。

题目问邮轮公司在社交媒体上使用的高质量宣传素材。原文第31句提到“employ top agencies to produce first rate videos for social media channels”，其中“first rate videos”对应题干中的“high quality ___”。根据“ONE WORD ONLY”要求，取核心名词“videos”作为答案，准确反映营销手段。

transcript.en: Part 4
You will hear part of a presentation by a tourism studies student on the cruise ship industry.
transcript.cn: 你将听到一位旅游研究学生关于邮轮行业的部分报告。

transcript.en: First, you have some time to look at questions 31 to 40.
transcript.cn: 首先，你有一些时间查看第31至第40题。

transcript.en: Now listen carefully and answer questions 31 to 40.
transcript.cn: 现在请仔细听，并回答第31至第40题。

transcript.en: For my presentation today I'm going to talk about some of the issues facing the cruise ship industry and then some ways these can be addressed.
transcript.cn: 今天的报告中，我将谈谈邮轮行业面临的一些问题，以及可以应对这些问题的一些方法。

transcript.en: The cruise ship industry has partly been responsible for the effects of overtourism in recent decades. Overtourism occurs in places where excessive numbers of tourists cause significant problems.
transcript.cn: 邮轮行业近几十年来对过度旅游的影响负有一定责任。过度旅游发生在游客数量过多导致严重问题的地方。

transcript.en: Pollution, for example, is among the greatest threats to many popular tourist destinations.
transcript.cn: 例如，污染是许多热门旅游目的地面临的最大威胁之一。

transcript.en: Of course, for many places it's a difficult balance to achieve.
transcript.cn: 当然，对许多地方来说，这是一个难以实现的平衡。

transcript.en: They want to promote their city or island as a desirable tourist destination, but at the same time, are unable to cope with thousands of cruise ship passengers on a daily basis.
transcript.cn: 他们希望将自己的城市或岛屿推广为理想的旅游目的地，但同时，又无法每天应对成千上万的邮轮乘客。

transcript.en: The trouble is, excessive tourism is destroying the beauty spots and places of interest that people come to visit.
transcript.cn: 问题是，过度旅游正在破坏人们前来参观的美景和名胜古迹。

transcript.en: Several cities, such as Barcelona, have responded by imposing a tax which all visitors to the city from cruise ships have to pay.
transcript.cn: 一些城市，如巴塞罗那，通过征收所有邮轮游客必须缴纳的税款来应对这一问题。

transcript.en: But as it's only a couple of euros, many green campaigners think it won't deter enough people to make any difference.
transcript.cn: 但由于只是几欧元，许多环保人士认为这不足以阻止足够多的人从而产生任何变化。

transcript.en: Bruges is another city which became impossible to navigate at times because of the huge numbers of cruise passengers arriving on day trips from the port of Zeebrugge.
transcript.cn: 布鲁日是另一个因大量邮轮乘客从泽布吕赫港抵达进行一日游而有时变得难以通行的城市。

transcript.en: The city was becoming like a 'theme park', with shops only catering for tourists, selling chocolate, which Belgium is famous for, and other souvenirs. The local council took action to limit cruise passengers to a more manageable level.
transcript.cn: 这座城市正变得像一个「主题公园」，商店只迎合游客，出售比利时著名的巧克力和其他纪念品。当地议会采取行动将邮轮乘客限制在一个更可管理的水平。

transcript.en: Dubrovnik had to limit the numbers of cruise ships after it became extremely popular as a cruise ship destination when it featured in a hugely successful TV series.
transcript.cn: 杜布罗夫尼克在一部大获成功的电视剧中出现后，作为邮轮目的地变得极为热门，不得不限制邮轮数量。

transcript.en: What it does now is control the timing of all cruise ship entries to the city's port. However, many people feel this measure does not go far enough.
transcript.cn: 它现在所做的是控制所有邮轮进入城市港口的时间，然而，许多人认为这一措施还不够彻底。

transcript.en: Cruise ships may be unpopular in some of their destinations but they also have an image problem.
transcript.cn: 邮轮在某些目的地可能不受欢迎，但它们也存在形象问题。

transcript.en: They've always been perceived as a safe holiday for the elderly, with not much on offer for families or young couples.
transcript.cn: 它们一直被视为老年人的安全假期，对家庭或年轻夫妇来说没有太多可提供的内容。

transcript.en: A recent survey showed that cost is also a major factor in putting younger groups off going on a cruise.
transcript.cn: 最近的一项调查显示，费用也是让年轻群体对邮轮望而却步的主要因素。

transcript.en: But what they don't realise is that compared to other types of package holidays, cruises can actually be good value, as all activities and drinks are often included.
transcript.cn: 但他们没有意识到的是，与其他类型的套餐假期相比，邮轮实际上性价比很高，因为所有活动和饮料通常都包含在内。

transcript.en: And another perception is that cruises have lots of rules about what to wear and how to behave. But these days, most cruises are no longer very formal and behind the times.
transcript.cn: 另一个看法是，邮轮对着装和行为有很多规定。但如今，大多数邮轮不再那么正式和守旧了。

transcript.en: So what solutions are there for cruise lines to overcome some of these problems? How can they appeal to younger customers? Well one selling point is that cruise ships are becoming more sustainable.
transcript.cn: 那么邮轮公司有哪些解决方案来克服这些问题？他们如何吸引年轻顾客？一个卖点是邮轮变得更加可持续。

transcript.en: New ships are built with hybrid engines with large batteries which means ships do not have to keep their engines running while docked.
transcript.cn: 新船配备了带有大型电池的混合动力发动机，这意味着船只在停靠时不必保持发动机运转。

transcript.en: Cruise lines are also designing ships specially for those in the age range of 21 to 45. The décor in these feels contemporary and there are a range of activities on board that you wouldn't find on a more traditional cruise.
transcript.cn: 邮轮公司还专门为21至45岁年龄段的人设计船只。这些船的装饰感觉很现代，船上有一系列在传统邮轮上找不到的活动。

transcript.en: There's even a boxing ring on one ship, and most offer diving expeditions. But there's also a huge focus on well-being with a variety of sessions of different kinds.
transcript.cn: 其中一艘船甚至有拳击台，大多数船提供潜水探险活动，同时也非常注重健康，提供各种不同类型的课程。

transcript.en: Food is always a very important part of any cruise and cruise ships have had to radically update their menus to suit the tastes of their younger customers.
transcript.cn: 食物始终是任何邮轮非常重要的一部分，邮轮公司不得不彻底更新菜单以满足年轻顾客的口味。

transcript.en: Vegan dishes are standard, for example. The restaurants on board have also gone paperless with menus available on screen.
transcript.cn: 例如，纯素菜肴已成为标准配置。船上的餐厅也已无纸化，菜单在屏幕上显示。

transcript.en: Unlike older generations who went on cruises largely to get away from everything, younger people expect to be able to keep in touch with friends and family.
transcript.cn: 与主要为了逃离一切而乘坐邮轮的老一代人不同，年轻人希望能够与朋友和家人保持联系。

transcript.en: Many people going on longer cruises also spend time working, so companies have to guarantee wifi that can be relied upon at all times.
transcript.cn: 许多乘坐较长行程邮轮的人也会花时间工作，因此公司必须保证随时可靠的WiFi。

transcript.en: My grandparents used to love looking through cruise brochures, even when they weren't planning on going on a cruise. Until very recently TV ads for cruises always felt dated and aimed at retirees.
transcript.cn: 我的祖父母过去常常喜欢翻阅邮轮宣传册，即使他们没有计划乘坐邮轮。直到最近，邮轮的电视广告总是感觉过时，针对退休人员。

transcript.en: Cruise lines have been slow to adopt the power of social media but that's all changing. Leading cruise lines now employ top agencies to produce first rate videos for social media channels.
transcript.cn: 邮轮公司一直缓慢地采用社交媒体的力量，但这一切都在改变。领先的邮轮公司现在聘用顶级机构为社交媒体渠道制作一流视频。

transcript.en: It will be interesting to see whether …
transcript.cn: 看看是否……将会很有趣。
